scaled_oracle_cases.description: Scaled compartments (10) and boundaries (9) with default penalties (all weights 1).
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Ensure function correctly handles large number of compartments and boundaries.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: The function must produce weights of 1 for all added reactions when no custom penalties are given.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: If function incorrectly assigns non-1 weights or misses reactions.
scaled_oracle_cases.call_code:
extended_model, weights = create_extended_model(model)
print('Extended model reactions:', extended_model.reactions)
print('Weights:', weights)
result = {'extended_model': extended_model, 'weights': weights}
covered_requirements: The scaled case must generate weights for all compartments and boundaries with default penalties.

scaled_oracle_cases.description: Test extraneous penalty keys in penalties dict with original model.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Check that the function includes all penalty entries even if reaction IDs were not added.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: The function must include all penalty entries in the weights dict, even if the reaction ID wasn't added by helper functions.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: If the function only assigns penalties to reactions that exist, the nonexistent keys would be missing from output.
covered_requirements: The scaled case must include the extraneous penalty keys in the output weights.
